response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Java, C/C++) and a track record of low-latency systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as primary, and the resume shows only limited recent C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and no Rust or geospatial routing/navigation tile pipeline experience. The closest adjacency is GIS work via GISCollective/Green Map, but that's web GIS, not routing tile production. Worth a screening call to assess C++ depth and willingness to ramp, but not a strong match.
strengths: Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective and Green Map open-source work
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, trunk-based development
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience since 2015; no Rust experience listed
No navigation/routing tile pipeline or map data transformation background
Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js, far from the C++/Linux systems focus of the role

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ / Rust
